posts.caption:
Milan Menswear Fashion Week SS’27 showcased a shift towards ease, quiet luxury and individuality. From sharper silhouettes to relaxed fabrics, these are the trends that defined the season ➡️
📷: Getty Images
Skinny Fit
After seasons dominated by oversized proportions, slimmer silhouettes are making a return. Think streamlined trousers, closer-fitting tailoring and a more defined shape.
Sheer & Skin-Baring Dressing
Designers embraced transparency and subtle sensuality through sheer fabrics, deep necklines, and bare torsos. The trend is less about exposure and more about adding lightness and confidence to a look.
Layering took on a more effortless approach this season. Jackets and tailored pieces over tanks and vests with easy combinations created depth and dimension without feeling over-styled.
Soft Luxury
Rather than loud logos or statement pieces, soft luxury focused on exceptional fabrics, elevated basics, and understated elegance. This brings in a polished yet relaxed wardrobe that feels timeless.
Shorts made their way back as a menswear essential, appearing in both casual and tailored looks. Used in relaxed silhouettes to refined styling, they offer a versatile approach to warm-weather dressing.
posts.alt_text:
Presenting one of our three Summer issue covers with the leading men of @theodysseymovie: Matt Damon as Odysseus. 

A Hollywood veteran of nearly five decades now, Damon has worked on approximately 80 film sets. And yet, as he told GQ’s @zachbaron, “Every single location on this movie would’ve been the hardest location on any other movie I’ve ever done… Every time we’d go somewhere, we’d be like, ‘Well, Iceland will be easier.’ And then it’s raining sideways and it’s fucking freezing. Iceland was like, ‘Yeah, easy? Hey, hold my beer.’”

For Damon, though, the challenge was part of what made the project so special: “All of the things that might have been difficult for me at an earlier point in my life weren’t difficult. They became fun. Like being wet and cold was not a hardship. It was actually something I felt really lucky to be experiencing. It felt temporary and like a gift.”

Damon unwraps the ‘gift’ that was making The Odyssey, alongside his co-stars Robert Pattinson and Tom Holland, and director Christopher Nolan, for GQ’s summer issue cover story at the 🔗 in bio.

Story by @zachbaron
Photographs by @alexprager
Styled by @georgecortina
Hair by @teddycharleshair at Nevermind
Skin by @jostrettell at Walter Schupfer Management
Set design by @joneslikeindiana

posts.caption:
As the World Cup continues, women across the country risk experiencing heightened violence at the hands of their abusers.
‘While we know that domestic abuse isn’t caused by football, we also know that heightened emotions and alcohol consumption can lead to a rise in domestic abuse frequency and severity,’ explains Farah Nazeer, CEO at Women’s Aid.
‘We need to call this out for what it is, which is misogyny,’ adds Emily. ‘It shouldn’t happen anywhere, whether it’s in a football context or not.’
Visit @solacewomensaid and @womens_aid to donate or if you need support.

posts.caption:
Fashion has no shortage of beautiful faces, but @alexconsani represents a new generation of supermodels—the ones not just walking the runway but creating platforms, building communities, and leading with personality. First gaining attention on TikTok, Consani is now one of the world’s most sought after models. “I’m having fun,” says Consani. “I’m doing it for all 50 of the other bitches that would love to be in that room.” For Bazaar’s Summer 2026 issue, the supermodel talks about trying her hand at acting, moving into her big-girl home, and entering her “experimental era.”

As the UK basks in a heatwave, we bring you a new edition of HELLO! magazine, HOT off the press. There is a full report on all the royal and celebrity appearances at the Ascot races where the Princess of Wales made a triumphant return after three years away. Keeping with the equestrian theme, we have an exclusive shoot and interview with former reality television star and dressage rider Gemma Owen, daughter of former footballer Michael, who talks all about her new challenges and what her dad thinks of her chosen sport. There is also a chat with DJ Fat Tony as he releases his memoirs and reveals his thoughts on the Beckham family feud, as well as a guide to this year’s World Cup WAGs 💫

posts.caption:
Things are getting interesting in the footwear department of @ysl, in a great way, of course.⁠
⁠
Saint Laurent's Design Director, @corrado777, takes to Instagram to tease a look at some of the more unique silhouettes from the Spring/Summer 2027 presentation at Paris Fashion Week. Swipe for a closer look.⁠

For Pre-Fall 2026, @moncler trades the summit for the quad, drawing from academic libraries and university campuses. ⁠
⁠
The collection leans into intellectual dressing through meticulous layering, tactile fabric play, and close attention to detail. Technical outerwear remains at the core, but it's reframed through a collegiate lens, with a preppier look. The collection balances Moncler’s heritage that we know and love with a bit of refinement.⁠
⁠
Shop the entire Moncler Pre-Fall 2026 collection via select Moncler retailers and their online shop.
posts.alt_text: That’s one way to deal with the heat wave in Paris

posts.caption:
For the tenth volume of their collaboration, @stussy and @ourlegacy return to what they do best: making the familiar feel entirely new. ⁠
⁠
Reworked by hand between Los Angeles and Stockholm, each piece carries its own distinct character, embracing imperfections, variations, and the kind of individuality that only comes from human touch. Designed to evolve with wear, colors are expected to fade, shift, and bleed over time, allowing the collection to develop alongside its owner. ⁠
⁠
With each piece being a 1-of-1, quantities are extremely limited. Shop the entire collection now via Stüssy and Our Legacy.

posts.caption:
With Europe in the grip of a heat wave, many Milan Fashion Week attendees turned to shorts and athletic-inspired dressing. @giorgioarmani offered a different solution. On Sunday, men’s creative director Leo Dell’Orco presented a breezy collection built around soft tailoring, sun-faded hues and lightweight fabrics including linen, washed silk and treated denim.⁠
⁠
Inspired by the Mediterranean, the collection featured relaxed silhouettes, worn-in textures and tonal shades of sage, beige, brown and blue. Standout pieces included pale denim shirts and bleached denim tailoring, proving that even in soaring temperatures, elegance doesn't have to give way to practicality.⁠

posts.caption:
Memphis Depay has never been defined by football alone.
In the city he now calls home, the Netherlands’ (@onsoranje) all-time leading scorer reflects on life in Brazil, discusses the identity he’s built beyond the pitch, shares why music has become a vital form of self-expression, and introduces his four-legged co-star Drako, who makes a cameo of his own.
Head to the link in bio to read our June digital cover story with @memphisdepay, where we talked fashion, creativity, and staying true to himself just days before his World Cup journey started.
posts.alt_text:
Introducing our June digital cover star, @memphisdepay.

To some, he’s one of football’s greatest forwards. To others, he’s a musician, designer, all-around entrepreneur and icon who has spent his entire career challenging the idea that athletes should stay in a single lane.

Ahead of what could be his final World Cup, Memphis reflects on identity, creativity, criticism and why football has always been only one part of a much larger story. “My name is bigger than football in what I live, what I do, and what I represent,” he tells us.

Head to the link in our bio to read the full cover story.

posts.caption:
Producer, DJ, electronic artist and culture shaker Fred again.. (@fredagainagainagainagainagain) is finally coming to India in what will be his only tour this year !
After much speculation and teasing with newspaper front pages across Delhi, Mumbai and Bangalore Fred has finally confirmed the three city tour this December on his Instagram along with India exclusive merch designed by Diya Joukani (@diyajoukani) and tour poster designed by @painterkafeelartist . The artist will perform at Leisure Valley Ground in Delhi NCR on December 5, lMahalaxmi Race Course in Mumbai on December 9, and NICE Grounds in Bengaluru on December 13. Tickets go on sale on June 30 exclusively for those who pre-register through BookMyShow (@bookmyshowin).
[Fred again.., India tour 2026, Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore, Music events in India]

posts.caption:
When I walk out of the India exclusive screening of ‘The Furious,’ which is being called the best action film of the year; two thoughts enter my brain. The first thought is that – ‘The Furious’ is bringing action we’ve never seen before to the cinemas. The second thought is that – my dad would’ve loved this film.
‘The Furious’ lands at that perfect moment when CGI fatigue, and AI has entered the film making conversation with an answer that’s almost embarrassingly simple: hire people who can actually fight, point the camera at them, and show them doing what they do best. It worked for Bruce Lee. It worked for Tony Jaa flinging himself through barbed wire in Ong-Bak. And it’s working now because ‘The Furious’ doesn’t plan on reinventing Kung Fu film’s grammar it’s just reminding us of it.
At the link in bio, Chirrag Guha (@guha.archie) decodes the impact of ‘The Furious’ on modern day action films, Gen Z’s pallete for old school martial arts and why its not made its mark in the Indian box office yet.
[The Furious, Kenji Tanigaki, action movies]

posts.caption:
@jennifer.garner *always* knows what to do (and it usually involves sorghum).
Between starring in summer’s buzziest new series, taking her near-billion dollar company public, and turning ‘Pretend Cooking Show’ into a reality, she still manages to be the nicest woman in Hollywood.
Playing a widowed food blogger in @Peacock’s adaptation of @elinhilderbrand’s bestseller ‘The Five-Star Weekend,’ the beloved actress and entrepreneur channels her inner Ina Garten for a not-so-Instagrammable girls’ weekend on Nantucket with co-stars-turned-IRL besties, @chloesevigny, @morereginahall, @gemmachan and @darcycarden, exploring what it means to grow and develop in middle age.
At 54, the Marvel Hero and supermom reflects on her genre-spanning career, being “total besties” with John Cena, her surprisingly vast knowledge of weaponry, plus the responsibility of celebrities to stand up for working locally for InStyle’s Summer Imagemakers Issue at the link in @instylemagazine’s bio now.
InStyle Summer, 2026

posts.alt_text:
@jennifer.garner *always* knows what to do (and it usually involves sorghum). 

Between starring in summer’s buzziest new series, taking her near-billion dollar company public, and turning ‘Pretend Cooking Show’ into a reality, she still manages to be the nicest woman in Hollywood. 

Playing a widowed food blogger in @Peacock’s adaptation of @elinhilderbrand’s bestseller ‘The Five-Star Weekend,’ the beloved actress and entrepreneur channels her inner Ina Garten for a not-so-Instagrammable girls’ weekend on Nantucket with co-stars-turned-IRL besties, @chloesevigny, @morereginahall, @gemmachan and @darcycarden, exploring what it means to grow and develop in middle age.

At 54, the Marvel Hero and supermom reflects on her genre-spanning career, being “total besties” with John Cena, her surprisingly vast knowledge of weaponry, plus the responsibility of celebrities to stand up for working locally for InStyle’s Summer Imagemakers Issue at the link in @instylemagazine’s bio now.

posts.caption:
All of our pleas to put @jordynwoods' lucky bag in a museum were finally heard! It was announced today that Jordyn's infamous @woodsbyjordyn bag will officially be on display at the Guggenheim for a special 5-day exhibit starting June 24th. When asked about lending her bag to the museum Jordyn stated, “I never imagined that something I designed would one day be on view at the museum. So many of us are still in shock over the Knicks’ historic run, and seeing the lucky bag at the Guggenheim somehow makes it all feel real.”
📷: Courtesy of The Solomon R Guggenheim Foundation

posts.caption: Brother or Sister I don’t take your time lightly as I only seek to use it wisely for the purpose of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ who is soon to get his church. I pray that your faith and love in this world is built on the foundation of Christ Jesus. It is the only name with power and authority. No one disrespects Buda, Mohammed or any other deity the way they do Jesus, or is persecuted more than Christians.The church is asleep and whether you believe in a pre tribulation rapture or post tribulation I warn you that the Bible states we are not appointed to wrath only disobedient people are. The question is not do you know God but does God know you? Do you give up the things of this world to spend time in his presence? Do you keep from idolatry, fornication,lust, adultery,masturbation and everything the Bible warns off. Are you walking in the Spirit are you working out your salvation with fear and trembling. God is about to pour out his wrath where would you go if today was your last day? We are living in the last days make time to know your Creator and his son who gave his life so you could have life.

posts.caption:
Yes, it’s L’OFFICIEL! Cosa hanno in comune “Dune: Part Two”, “Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga” e la nuova serie Apple TV “Lucky”? Sono tutti ambientati nel deserto—e tutti vedono protagonista @AnyaTaylorJoy. «Mi diverte sempre, perché non capisco cosa ci sia nei miei capelli, nel mio incarnato o nella larghezza dei miei occhi che mi renda adatta a interpretare una creatura del deserto. Eppure la gente adora mettermi lì», confessa Taylor-Joy a L’OFFICIEL. «Avverto una vera affinità con questi mondi».
Dal pianeta brullo di Arrakis in “Dune” al Wasteland che domina “Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga”, la nostra protagonista di giugno continua a ritrovarsi negli scenari più ostili del cinema contemporaneo. «Il deserto possiede una sacralità propria», osserva. Alla domanda su cosa ami di questi spazi desolati, la risposta è immediata: «L’alba e il tramonto, soprattutto. Perché vedi i colori più straordinari e senti di essere l’unica persona sulla terra».
Global Chief Content Officier @giampietrobaudo

posts.caption:
Introducing our global cover star, @anyataylorjoy.
“I’ve always been nomadic by nature,” the British-Argentine actor tells L’OFFICIEL. “Now I’ve just fully embraced it as part of who I am. If I have a couple of days off, I’m always like, ‘Ooh, what’s within driving distance?’ If it’s six hours or less, I’m like, ‘Let’s go. Why not?’”
Fresh off of a recent desert road trip, Taylor-Joy—who has cemented her status as a fashion darling through ambassadorships with @Dior and @TiffanyandCo—is preparing for the release of Apple TV’s ‘Lucky,’ a thriller about a con artist caught in the fallout of a multimillion-dollar heist gone wrong. The role marks another chapter in a career spanning ‘The Witch,’ ‘The Queen’s Gambit,’ ‘Furiosa,’ and ‘Dune.’
In ‘Lucky,’ her character is pursued by the FBI and a ruthless mob boss while searching for something more elusive than freedom: stability. “I was so attracted to playing somebody who was just so desperate to stay still and put down roots,” says Taylor-Joy.
Filmed across the desert and inside Las Vegas’s Caesars Palace, the production left its mark. “I have a real soft spot for Caesars Palace now—we’ve been through a lot together,” she laughs.
At the link in bio, read our full conversation with Taylor-Joy on inhabiting new characters, life on the road, and ‘Lucky.’

posts.caption:
Da oggi e fino al 28 giugno, la capitale francese accende le sue passerelle per la Paris Fashion Week dedicata all’uomo — la tappa finale e più lunga del mese della moda maschile, dopo Firenze e Milano, con le collezioni Primavera/Estate 2027. Si apre stasera con il consueto blockbuster di @LouisVuitton alle 21, firmato @Pharrell. A seguire, sei giorni di sfilate tra grandi maison e nuove voci: @Dior con la terza collezione uomo di Jonathan Anderson, @YSL, e poi Rick Owens, Yohji Yamamoto, Celine, Sacai, KidSuper. La moda uomo trova a Parigi il suo gran finale.
Photo: Getty Images
#PFW #MensFashionWeek #LOfficiel #LOfficielItalia

posts.caption:
Leo Dell’Orco e Silvana Armani firmano la collezione uomo @GiorgioArmani Primavera/Estate 2027, affiancata da una selezione di look della prima collezione donna Cruise 2027 di Silvana. Un debutto che è anche un atto d’amore: portare avanti la grammatica di Re Giorgio. La spalla scolpita e morbida, i toni greige, la sartorialità che respira. Senza imitarla, ma abitandola dall’interno. Due persone che hanno lavorato accanto a lui per una vita intera, oggi custodi di una visione che ha cambiato per sempre il modo di vestire. Il maestro non c’è più, ma la sua linea resta.
Photo: Getty Images
#GiorgioArmani #MFW #LOfficiel #LOfficielItalia

posts.caption:
Welcome to the June issue, where L’OFFICIEL explores travel not as movement, but as a state of mind. Our “nomadic by nature” cover star @AnyaTaylorJoy discusses her love of road trips, filming the new Apple TV series ‘Lucky’ in the Las Vegas desert, and her unique approach to learning her characters. The enigmatic photographer @SzilveszterMako, renowned for his surreal images, cinematic backdrops, and dreamlike scenes, turned his lens on the actress, capturing her shifting world for our shoot.
Elsewhere, the issue focuses on slow travel and the art of pausing. We take you to Grand Cayman, Ischia, and Costa Rica, where hydrotherapy and relaxation define the experience, and across the Canadian Rockies, Arabian Desert, and Australian Outback by rail—the defining way to travel in 2026. Elsewhere, we follow one writer into the next frontier of luxury wellness: sleep optimization.
In Paris, we sit down with Hong Kong–born couturier @RobertWun, who has made a name for himself with dramatic, over-the-top silhouettes; he reminds us that slowing down is sometimes the most radical imaginative act. And in Tbilisi, it’s the creative class who are putting the Georgian capital on the map—and we have your guide to everything that’s happening now.
On the fashion front, Spring/Summer 2026’s finest looks head to Cape Town for a sun-drenched escape, while long-heralded summer heroes—unexpected denim and easy dresses—capture the spirit of going nowhere in good company, this time in Los Angeles’s canyons. Stay tuned for our issue to hit newsstands this month.

posts.caption:
June isn’t quite over yet, and summer is just getting started—Cape Town, South Africa, is still on our minds.
Between ocean glare and mountain silhouettes, the city moves in easy contrast. Beaches fade into busy streets, palm-lined corners catch the light, and everything feels a little slowed down. For Pre-Fall 2026, we’re dressing into that in-between feeling—slipping through the city in pieces that match the mood, from a @Chanelofficial tiger-print dress to a silky orange @Dior silhouette. Explore Cape Town through L’OFFICIEL’s lens at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
@alexandermcqueen is coming home! After years outside the British capital, the House of McQueen has confirmed its return to the official London Fashion Week schedule for Spring/Summer 2027, marking #SeánMcGirr’s first runway in London since taking over the house.
The last time #AlexanderMcQueen showed in London was Fall/Winter 2001, ‘What a Merry-Go-Round’-his final London collection before the move to Paris Fashion Week. Built around a fully functioning merry-go-round, the show transformed the runway into an immersive set piece that quickly moved far from any traditional “carnival” mood.
What appeared playful on the surface became something far more unsettling, rooted in the darker, macabre side of childhood fantasy and circus imagery. When the show began, models stormed the space with intensity, moving like a fierce force rather than a conventional runway cast. The tone shifted immediately-nothing about it was candy-coated or nostalgic.
A commanding cast moved through the installation in patent leather, structured tailoring, and sharply cut silhouettes, set against flashes of exposed skin, sheer fabrics, and distorted takes on glamour. Visit the link in our bio for more.
Photos Getty Images
#mcqueen #leemcqueen #fashionhistory

posts.caption:
@Balenciaga dropped its Spring Summer 2026 preview collection in Mumbai last night, and the energy in the room felt different.
With Fatima Sana Sheikh, Bobby Deol, Mouni Roy, Sanya Malhotra, Malavika Mohanan, and Krithi Shetty in attendance, it felt less like a formal showcase and more like a real moment around fashion. The room was sharp, observant, and engaged.
Fatima Sana Shaikh said it best: “Balenciaga always has a chic approach and the brand is now heading towards it even more aggressively.” And that tone carried through the evening. Nothing felt loud for attention, yet everything commanded it.
What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.
That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it.
@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
Jacob & Co. continues to blur the line between watchmaking, sculpture, and fantasy with the Astronomia “Gates of Olympus.”
Priced at $1.4 million, the one-of-one creation draws inspiration from Greek mythology, featuring Zeus, Olympus, celestial movement, and a fully mechanical miniature world built directly into the timepiece itself.
Inside the 18K rose gold case sits a triple-axis tourbillon, over 9 carats of baguette diamonds, and an intricate Olympus scene designed as part of the movement.
More than a watch, the piece reflects how modern haute horology is increasingly moving toward collectible art and immersive storytelling.
@JacobAndCo #JacobAndCo #Luxurious #Collab

posts.caption:
Burberry has transformed Shanghai’s Plaza 66 into an immersive garden-inspired experience, bringing a touch of the English countryside to one of China’s most important luxury destinations.
Inspired by traditional English gardens, the pop-up features a hedge maze, Burberry statues, and the brand’s signature Equestrian Knight brought to life through a striking water feature. Brand ambassador Wu Lei joined friends of the house to celebrate the opening.
Open until August 31, the installation reflects Burberry’s growing focus on experiential retail, creating destinations that go beyond shopping and immerse visitors in the brand’s world.
Images: @Burberry

For India’s wealthiest families, education is about more than academics. It’s about access, networks, legacy, and opportunity.
From historic boarding schools that educated royalty and prime ministers to modern international campuses attended by celebrity and business families, these institutions have become some of the most sought-after addresses in Indian education.
Here are the elite schools where many of India’s most influential families choose to educate the next generation.
Images: Courtesy of Respective Schools

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.
For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.
“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.
Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”
Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.
Chairperson: Avarna Jain (@avarna_jain)
CEO: Debashish Ghosh
Editor: Aindrila Mitra (@aindrilamitra)
Interview: Akshay Kaushal (@akshay_kaushal)
Photography: Vaishnav Praveen (@vaishnavpraveen)
Styling: Mohit Rai (@mohitrai)
Fashion assistant: Shubhi Kumar (@shubhi.kumar)
Hair: Rohit Bhatkar (@rohit_bhatkar)
Makeup: Riviera Lynn (@rivieralynn)
Video: Kris Arshad (@itskrisarshad)
PR Agency: Tree-Shul Media (@treeshulmediasolutions)
Artist management: Benchmark Talents (@benchmarktalents)
Production: Studio Little Dumpling (@studiolittledumpling)

#Manifest #manifestcover #rukminivasanth
posts.alt_text:
No infinity pools. No staged romance. No “do it for the Gram” moments. Just ancient Silk Route trails, star-filled skies, hot springs, folk stories, candlelit dinners, and the kind of quality time that’s becoming increasingly rare.

Tucked away in Ladakh’s Nubra Valley, this is the kind of honeymoon for couples who love collecting stories for their journal. The best honeymoons aren’t always the most luxurious. Just uninterrupted time together.

Read more via the link in bio 🔗
By Rituparna Som

#Manifest
[Ladakh, Nubra Valley, Honeymoon Inspiration, Couple Travel, Slow Travel, Luxury Travel, Romantic Getaways, Destination Honeymoon, Travel Trends, Couple Goals 2026]

Love may start the partnership, but financial clarity is what sustains it.

#ManifestCover

For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.

“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.

Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”

Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.

Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Every sign has a relationship wishlist sitting deep-down somewhere in the back of their mind. It has emotional needs, commitment issues, and dreams they’re not always ready to admit out loud. Time to let your zodiac expose the truth.
Everyone says they’re chill, unbothered, and just “going with the flow,” but underneath, every zodiac sign is quietly hoping for something specific in love. The good news? Knowing what you want isn’t needy—it’s the first step toward finding a relationship that actually feels right.
Your zodiac sign has entered the chat. And it’s ready to spill.
#Manifest #Horoscope

posts.caption:
Remember how things looked in 2016? Brands like Warby Parker and Everlane introduced themselves in tidy, sans-serif typefaces. Pastels, especially millennial pink, surged onto Instagram feeds and Glossier mascara tubes.
Lately, something misshapen and unruly has been rising in its place. Let’s call it hyper goo: a glitchy, gloppy, maximalist mash-up of surreal smoothness and digital degradation. Companies trying to reach younger audiences are turning to colorful, inflated-looking typography that recalls the 1970s and pixelated nods to the 1990s internet.
The effect is both nostalgic and playful, offering a tempting alternative to the disillusionment that comes with being a young person today. But there may be another reason this irreverent style has struck a chord: the rise of A.I.
Shreya Murthy, the chief executive of Partiful, the event-invitation service beloved by Gen Z, said that “for us and for a lot of other companies, we think that loud and messy and customizable and, honestly, an unserious aesthetic, that’s how you prove a human actually made this.” Read more about how hyper goo is transforming consumer culture at the link in our bio.
posts.alt_text:
As a designer of women’s clothing for 15 years, Simone Rocha has been committed to presenting a version of womanhood that subverts traditional feminine tropes, a practice she calls “pretty and perverse.” But as the culture reaches its peak “masculinity crisis” moment, @simonerocha_ is now turning more of her attention to men’s wear — focusing more on men’s softer side.

“When I started thinking about the men’s very seriously, I wanted it to feel grounded but also quite tender,” she told the New York Times reporter @yolamzizi.

Rocha has been designing men’s clothing since 2022, though until now, they have mostly served as counterpart to the women’s collections. Now she's giving men's wear its own space. This week, Rocha presented her first solo men’s show at Pitti Immagine Uomo, the biannual Italian trade show for men’s clothing. Will men bite?

Tap the link in our bio to read our full profile of Rocha and learn more about the collection came together. Photo by @jeremiesouteyrat

posts.caption:
Knicks fans showed up in all sorts of creative combinations of orange and blue during the ticker-tape parade on Thursday, but perhaps no one turned the merch into quite as much of a fashion statement as Rama Duwaji, the first lady of the city.
Making a rare public appearance with Mayor Zohran Mamdani — himself in a Josh Hart jersey under his suit jacket, with a polka-dot orange tie — Duwaji chose a one-shouldered peplum top by Claire Sullivan, an emerging New York designer. It was made from upcycled Knicks T-shirts in white, orange and blue, and she wore it with a full black skirt, orange pompom earrings and black lace-ups with ankle socks. But the top, which was a sort of downtown Les Miz-meets-DIY-meets-team spirit confection, was the point.
Tap the link in our bio to read more about how @ramaduwaji's parade look came together. Photo by Yuki Iwamura/@apnews

posts.caption:
After tabloid reports claimed Taylor Swift would wed Travis Kelce on June 13 in Watch Hill, Rhode Island, fans descended upon the town hoping for a glimpse of the singer.
The reports, though unconfirmed, seemed plausible, some fans said. The singer, a numerology fanatic, has a longstanding fondness for the number 13; a June wedding would work with Kelce’s offseason; and, perhaps most convincingly, Swift has owned a home, known as Holiday House, in Watch Hill since 2013.
While no Swifts were to be found last weekend, there were, however, plenty of Swifties. Fans of all ages, including sisters, mother-daughter duos and one group on a trip with “three generations of Swifties,” embraced the charms of the town. Read more about how Swift fans spent their weekend in Watch Hill at the link in our bio. Photos by @sarahmeftah

posts.caption:
The fifth annual Las Culturistas Culture Awards, created and hosted by the comedians Bowen Yang and Matt Rogers, were held at the United Theater on Broadway in Los Angeles on May 30. The show aired last night on Bravo and is now streaming on Peacock.
An offshoot of the hosts’ popular “Las Culturistas” podcast, the celebrity-packed comedy special is both a parody of the self-seriousness of awards shows and a loving tribute to the nooks and crannies of pop culture that traditional ceremonies ignore. Spanning more than 100 categories, the awards recognized humans, foods, cartoons and abstract ideas.
Stacey Rusch of “The Real Housewives of Potomac” beat Mayor Zohran Mamdani for best new artist. Ciara Miller, a fan favorite from “Summer House,” won this year’s “Allison Williams cool girl award.” Eric Nam, a singer and recent contestant on “The Traitors,” won most surprising snack, toppling competitors that included “Big thing of cottage cheese.”
“Here at the Culture Awards,” Yang stressed onstage, “there is no such thing as high or low culture. Everything is just culture.” At the link in our bio, read more about the Culture Awards winners and see more photos from inside the show (and the after-party). Photos by Bravo, Rodin Eckenroth/Getty Images, @alexwelshphoto

posts.caption:
Last week, Ellie the Elephant, beloved mascot of the New York Liberty, celebrated her sixth birthday party. It was a bash involving dance routines and costume changes, held in front of 17,000 people at Barclays Center. Many elements of Ellie’s birthday outfit and party were provided by sponsors. But to her fans, Ellie is far more than a billboard.
Over the past six years, Ellie’s distinctive aesthetic and courtside panache have allowed many Black women, Liberty fans or not, to see themselves in her. In performance, she has been known to channel divas like Mary J. Blige, Lil’ Kim and Whitney Houston. “Ellie represents culture,” Criscia Long, senior director of entertainment for the Liberty, told the New York Times reporter @s_evangelina.
At the link in our bio, see more scenes from @bigellieliberty’s birthday bash and read more about what the mascot means to fans. Visuals by @djdumpling

posts.caption:
After almost five years in Paris, #AlexanderMcQueen has announced that it will be returning home, bringing its Spring/Summer 2027 show back to #LondonFashionWeek this September.
A true homecoming, some of Lee’s most extraordinary runway finales were created in #London, the city where his vision first captivated the fashion world. An emotional climax of the story, His shows often ended with moments that blurred the boundaries between fashion, theatre, performance art and technology, leaving audiences stunned and creating images that are still discussed decades later.
posts.alt_text:
One of my favourite, and most hauntingly beautiful details from #DanielRoseberry’s #Schiaparelli couture collections was the sheer crinoline veils/face masks, from Fall 2024. Wrapped around the face and head they functioned as sculptural forms. The material appeared almost weightless, creating distortions that blurred the contours of the face and transformed the models into embryonic works of art. Makeup by @patstingz, hair by @guidopalau 

Also reminded me of #GarethPugh’s Spring 2013 collection, which featured sheer mesh and stretched fabrics wrapped around the head and face, or #MarcJacobs Spring 2019. Major inspo for brides looking for an unconventional wedding veil!

posts.caption:
For the grand finale of her wedding to #CallumTurner, #DuaLipa looked to none other than #Chanel for her wedding dress. The custom #HauteCouture creation marked a historic moment for the house as the first-ever bridal look by #MatthieuBlazy.
The dress which was made in Paris was entirely hand-embroidered with 480,000 beads by @ateliermontex. It featured a two-meter train which was underlined with 25,000 feathers by @maison_lemarie
Images courtesy of @davidsimsofficial
posts.alt_text:

posts.caption:
#Balenciaga Spring 2027 may be #PierpaoloPiccioli’s clearest vision for the brand yet. Less about direct references, it focuses on #CristóbalBalenciaga’s philosophy of construction and the relationship between fabric and the body.
References to the house’s historic balloon, drape, and cocoon shapes suggest a welcome return to its heritage, but reengineered for modern life. Through new textile innovations, the collection emphasises lightness, with entire looks weighing less than a kilogram. @pppiccioli wants to demonstrate how #HauteCouture principles can be relevant to everyday life. Ordinary wardrobe staples are elevated through couture-level construction.
What do you think of the new Balenciaga so far?

posts.caption:
I fear #ChristianLouboutin has taken the whimsy trend to a whole new level, transforming one of the most iconic visual gags (the banana peel slip) into a shoe.
Made of yellow satin, the $1,995 heels feature hand-sculpted leather panels which curl around the foot to resemble the folds of a peeled banana. The design is a homage to the footwear of #IsabelCanovas who became renowned for transforming fruit, vegetables, flowers, shells, and other everyday objects into wearable sculptures. Much like her creations, the #Louboutin heel elevates something ordinary and  slightly absurd into luxury. #Shoes #FootwearDesign
Images courtesy of @styledbyjovon_ @christianlouboutin & The Metropolitan Museum of Art
posts.alt_text:
After almost five years in Paris, #AlexanderMcQueen has announced that it will be returning home, bringing its Spring/Summer 2027 show back to #LondonFashionWeek this September.

A true homecoming, some of Lee’s most extraordinary runway finales were created in #London, the city where his vision first captivated the fashion world. An emotional climax of the story, His shows often ended with moments that blurred the boundaries between fashion, theatre, performance art and technology, leaving audiences stunned and creating images that are still discussed decades later.

posts.caption:
First debuted for Fall 2024, the Robe Spirale was a feat of fashion engineering. At its core was a 3D-printed structure formed as one continuous coiled strip. Like a slap bracelet, it wrapped and snapped around the torso, using the natural curves of the body to hold itself securely in place.
#PieterMulier further developed the idea for Spring 2025 with a series of pleated dresses that appeared to float on the body. They gripped the torso at carefully calculated points, while hidden internal armatures carried much of the structural load. The visible fabric acted as the skin of the construction, and the lightweight pleating reduced the need for heavy internal support.
Just like #AzzedineAlaïa, @pieter_mulier obsesses with the idea of construction and rhe body merging fashion, sculpture and architecture engineering.

posts.caption:
Justin Bieber for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!
This is a real piece of history for us all. The past 10 years of lessons and learning have led up to this moment and I couldn’t be happier to share it with you all.
Some of you may be new followers who discovered us last week and some of you may have been here since day 1, either way this is for everyone who aligns with what it means to be an outlander.
Also, this is a great time to introduce myself for the first time in 10 years. My name is @callum.mccaff & we are all Outlander. I was born in 1996 so we priced the magazine at £19.96.
Available worldwide at www.outlandermag.com
PRINT ISN’T DEAD.
🫂 @lilbieber
📸 @sweetcian
🔗 @freenjoy
posts.alt_text:
Naomi Osaka for Outlander Magazine wearing her custom Nike Roland Garros looks designed by Nike & Kevin Germanier!

Roland Garros’ best dressed just raised the bar again. Naomi’s custom Nike looks were designed by Kevin Germanier, the couture designer turning upcycled materials into something iconic. Clay court season needed a moment like this, so we took her to an authentic orange clay court in paris to match the energy she’ll be surrounded by this week😤🤝

posts.caption:
Travis Scott for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!
GANG, TRAVIS SCOTT IN HERMÈS FOR OUR FIRST EVER MAGAZINE COVER. THIS IS UNHEARD OF, AND BETTER YET? IT WAS FOR OUTLANDER MAGAZINE. I AM HUMBLED.
WOW, what a moment. My only wish in this life is that what I’ve spent the last 10 years building acts as a strong form of inspiration for your own creative careers - I came from no fashion background, I had no connections & I had no idea what I was doing, but, I did it anyway.
If you didn’t know by now, my name is @callum.mccaff , I founded Outlander - and I’m here to remind everyone that PRINT ISN’T DEAD.
Available worldwide at www.outlandermag.com

posts.caption:
India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.
Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.
With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.
Read full interview at www.shethepeople.tv. | @renee.noronha
#Ironman #Philippines #sports full Ironman challenge, women in sports, women in endurance sports, mumbai sports, Chennai sports, IIT Madras student, women in STEM, ironman Kona, triathlon, marathon, menstrual awareness

FIFA has officially selected and accredited Assamese photojournalist Gitika Talukdar to cover the upcoming 2026 FIFA World Cup. This will be her third time covering the Men's FIFA World Cup.

Prior to this, Gitika has covered the 2018 Russia World Cup, the 2022 Qatar FIFA World Cup, the 2019 France Women's FIFA World Cup, the 2023 Women's FIFA World Cup in Australia and New Zealand, the 2020 Tokyo Olympic Games, the Paris Olympic Games, and others.

posts.caption:
A Phulkari dupatta, woven with generations of love before it ever touched her shoulders.
There’s a kind of tradition that doesn’t need explaining - it’s felt the moment you wear it. Rooted in Punjabi heritage, carried through hands that worked it stitch by stitch, and worn at exactly the right moment to begin exactly the right chapter. A Mata Ki Chowki hosted with love, and an outfit chosen not for how it would look, but for what it would mean.
Love. Family. Blessings. Nothing more was needed.
And then there was them. ❤️
But what made this celebration truly beautiful was how deeply personal every choice felt - a lehenga so detailed it reads like wearable art, an heirloom dupatta carrying blessings passed down through generations of women, and a bride who chose heritage over spectacle without ever losing an ounce of grandeur.
Some bridal looks are designed to impress.
And some are designed to mean something - every thread, every embellishment, every detail chosen with intention, and worn the way only a love story this considered deserves to be. ✨
Bride: @anshulakapoor
Outfit: @itrhofficial
Styled by: @mohitrai @ruchikrishnastyles
Jewellery: @khuranajewelleryhouse via @amigos.rizwan

Bag: @meera_mahadevia via @amigos.rizwan

posts.caption:
“Can we add just 10 more guests?”
Famous last words.
Because in weddings, there’s no such thing as “just 10 guests.” That’s 10 more meals. 10 more guest IDs. 10 more welcome hampers. 10 more rooms to figure out. 10 more transport arrangements to make.
Every small change creates a ripple effect. And if your team isn’t organised to handle it, that ripple becomes a wave.
That’s the difference between planning a wedding and understanding how weddings actually work.
Chekhmate helps your team stay organised as guest lists evolve and plans change  because in weddings, small changes are rarely small. ♟️✨

posts.caption:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.
It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.
And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.
Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.
Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.
That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.
➡️➡️ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.
Save this. Send it to a stylist who needs to hear it.
posts.alt_text:
Jonathan Anderson just showed his first couture collection for Dior. If you do not understand why it mattered, this post is for you.

John Galliano was front row. The artisans were the opening act. The silhouettes came from ceramicist Magdalene Odundo.

If you are in Paris: the show space at Musée Rodin opens to the public from 28 January for one week. The new couture collection, archival Dior pieces, and the Odundo ceramics that inspired it. You can see it yourself.

This is not about liking or not liking a collection. This is about understanding what you are looking at.
The Studio of Stylist Elixir is our online membership. Guides. Templates. Live calls. A community of stylists who are learning and building together. Comment ‘studio’ for a free trial.

Save this. Share it with someone who needs it.

#dior #jonathananderson #stylistelixir

posts.caption:
Hey everyone.
The pitch you keep not sending is not waiting for you to feel braver. It is just sitting there, costing you nothing and getting you nowhere.
A no does not take anything from you. It leaves you exactly where you already are, which is exactly where you are right now anyway. The only message that can actually change something is the one you send.
So pick the one that has been sitting in your drafts, or in your head, and send it today. Not when it is perfect. Today.
Then do the part most people skip, and follow up next week.

posts.caption:
Hey everyone.
My first portfolio was built on nothing. Borrowed clothes, a photographer who needed the work as much as I did, and a few looks literally held together with clips at the back.
A small budget is not a closed door. Money makes this easier, and pretending it does not helps no one, but I have seen the best portfolios come from resourceful stylists, not rich ones. Clients buy your eye, not your budget.
What is stopping your next shoot? Tell me below.
And if you want a real tip like this every morning, I send a free daily email made only for stylists. Comment PORTFOLIO and I will send you the link.
Day 13 of 30 of finding your dream clients🤍
#fashionstylist #personalstylist #fashionstyling #stylistportfolio #styling101

Hey everyone.
For years I thought loving clothes was the same as being a stylist. It is not. The job is an eye, a way of seeing you cannot switch off, and most of you already have it without giving yourself the title.
So go through them honestly.
Here is the hard part though. Having the eye is not the same as being booked for it. That gap is the whole thing, and it is exactly what we close inside The Studio.
How many of the five are you? Comment the number below, I read every one. And when you are ready to turn that eye into booked work, comment STUDIO and I will show you in.

posts.caption:
If there was any doubt that @thombrowne remains one of menswear’s great storytellers, his Milan return put it to rest. Staged in the courtyard of Palazzo Serbelloni beneath a sweltering Italian sun, Browne’s first standalone menswear show since 2023 was stunning.
Drawing inspiration from A Bug’s Life and the fairytale of the prince and the frog, Browne filled the runway with dragonflies, bees, spiders, crickets, and gilded beehives rendered through extraordinary embroidery and embellishment. Beneath the whimsy, however, was a collection firmly rooted in what he does best: impeccable tailoring, rigorous construction, and a deeply personal interpretation of American prep.
Images: Vogue; Backgrid
#ThomBrowne #MilanFashionWeek #Menswear #FashionWeek #TheCitizensPoste

What are all of the hot people with weird jobs and mysterious sources of income counting down to? Couture season, of course!!!
Godspeed to everyone with a press pass, a mood board, and an unexplained trust fund.
#CoutureWeek #Paris #FashionMonth #Moodboard #TheCitizensPoste

The Fashion Calendar can look like a mess out of context, but it is effectively the blueprint for the entire industry. And being able to understand and implement the language, systems, and strategy of it all is ultimately what’s going to keep any aspiring fashion professional in the game. If you can’t read “FW25 RTW” and know what you’re looking at (or ideally be able to picture the collection in your mind’s eye if you know the designer and director), it can be easy to feel like you’re lost.
And you can’t afford to get lost in fashion. So without further ado, here’s your cheat sheet to understanding how the Fashion Calendar actually works, all of its essential terminology, and why. And yes, I made a calendar chart you can save and reference.
Read the latest on Substack: The Citizen’s Poste | posteposteposte.com
#FashionCalendar #Substack #Couture #Menswear #FashionCapitals

Chanel has officially revealed the details behind Dua Lipa’s custom Haute Couture wedding gown, handcrafted in the ateliers at 31 Rue Cambon under the direction of Matthieu Blazy. The design took over 1,000 hours and brought together some of Chanel’s most celebrated Métiers d’Art, with Atelier Montex, Lemarié, and Lesage contributing intricate embellishments, while custom white satin pumps were created by Massaro.
And hey… the signs may have been there all along. Dua fronted the Chanel 25 campaign, has maintained a close relationship with the house for years, and famously wore vintage Chanel bridal-inspired couture to the 2021 Met Gala.
But most notably, this marks the first Chanel Haute Couture wedding dress ever designed by Matthieu Blazy, created not for a runway finale, but for a friend of the House.
#DuaLipa #ChanelBride #MatthieuBlazy #CallumTurner #Chanel

Motherhood is moving later because women have had to make room for everything else first: work, money, independence, partnership, choice.
IVF, egg freezing and better fertility care have made delayed parenthood more possible. Biology, however, has not adjusted its timeline.
For many women having children in their late 30s and early 40s, postpartum recovery can sit uncomfortably close to perimenopause. Fatigue, anxiety, sleep disruption and brain fog can start to look like motherhood, ageing, or both.
That is the blind spot. Women are expected to absorb hormonal change without always having the language, diagnosis or care to name what is happening.
The conversation around women’s health has to catch up with the reality of women’s lives.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia)

posts.caption:
Summer has always shaped life in India. What is changing is the extent to which life now has to be organised around heat.
People are leaving work earlier, staying out later, sleeping on floors, changing their diets, adjusting their school attendance, and spending more of their income on cooling.
These decisions are often framed as adaptation, yet many emerge from necessity rather than choice. For those who work outdoors, miss a day’s wage if they stay home, or cannot afford air conditioning, there is little room to negotiate with rising temperatures.
Heat leaves few dramatic images behind. It does not wash away roads or flatten buildings. Its effects accumulate in the body, in lost income, disrupted sleep, worsening health and shrinking margins for recovery.
As hotter days are followed by hotter nights, how long can individual households keep absorbing a crisis they did not create?
Read more at the link in bio.
By Arshia Dhar (@urseaa)
#HeatwaveIndia #ClimateCrisis #ExtremeHeat #ClimateChange #SummerInIndia

posts.caption:
Delhi’s microbrewery boom did not arrive overnight. Licensing delays, high production costs and a beer market long shaped by commercial labels meant the city came late to craft brewing.
That delay is now part of the story. Delhi’s newer microbreweries are not just putting wheat beer on tap; they are trying to build a more curious beer culture, one that makes room for seasonal brews, local ingredients, barrel-aged stouts, sours and beers with a point of view.
Craft beer is still far from the norm in India. But in Delhi-NCR, it is beginning to move beyond novelty, becoming part of a more experimental, experience-led way of drinking.
Read more on the link in bio.
By Karishma Kuenzang (@kkuenzang)
#CraftBeerIndia #MicrobreweriesInDelhi #DelhiBreweries #BeerCulture #TheEstablished

posts.caption:
Celebrity make-up artists in India have been shaping faces, trends and bridal moodboards for years. The beauty boom is here. The artist-led empire is still missing.
Globally, names like Charlotte Tilbury, Pat McGrath and Bobbi Brown turned artistry into authority, and authority into billion-dollar beauty businesses. In India, the same conversion has been slower. Local artists have built trust across skin tones, textures, climates and undertones, often doing the kind of real-world R&D global brands ignored for years.
They know what oxidises on brown skin. They know what collapses in Mumbai humidity. They know what survives a wedding, a shoot and a close-up.
Yet many remain tied to the wedding economy, where bridal bookings offer faster, steadier returns than the long, expensive work of building a brand. The result is a strange beauty paradox: India has the talent, the market and the demand. What it still lacks is the machinery.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia)
#makeupbrandsinindia #indianmakeupbrands #beautyindustry #celebritymakeupartists #colourcosmetics

posts.caption:
Fatherhood is a new role for the actor who first got noticed for his performance in ‘Masaan’ (2015). In fact, it seems this off-camera Vicky Kaushal (@vickykaushal09) is perhaps closer to Deepak Chaudhary, the character he played in the film. A sensitive, ambitious young man coming to terms with the world around him. That character, Kaushal says, changed him. But not for the obvious reasons.
“Until then success, to me, was where you reached [in life]. It was never about where you started from,” he explains. “That character, starting from the crematorium ghat in Benaras and going on to work as an engineer in the Railways in Allahabad [now Prayagraj]... He had a far bigger life arc than I’ve covered in my life. Till today,” he says, tapping the arm rest for emphasis. “After that I started having so much empathy for those around me, especially those from far less privileged backgrounds, because I don’t know where they have started from.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.
Editorial Direction: @meghamahindru @ridhimasapre

posts.caption:
Thirty minutes after this interview was supposed to begin, Vicky Kaushal’s (@vickykaushal09) tall, statuesque frame bursts in through the door at the Matrix office in Khar, Mumbai. “So sorry, bro,” he says, shaking this reporter’s hand with both of his. His day has been busy—his son’s visa was to be sorted, he couldn’t leave his wife alone, and there’s repair work going on in his building, he says, sounding like your average young husband and father with his daily household chores to take care of, not some Bollywood star with a platoon of staff to handle his every whim.
Since becoming a father, Kaushal says he’s come to understand and appreciate “a lot more” of the work his parents did in raising him. “It occurs to me at the most random moments that that [instilling the right values] is now my responsibility towards my son,” he says. “He is going to learn by observing how his parents are thinking, talking, and behaving. So, I’ve realised whatever you want to see reflected in him has to come in your actions rather than words.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
The current interest in ’90s-era windbreakers has little to do with the weather. It’s more about reliving a certain moment in time. The era of grainy VHS aerobics tapes and high-saturation music videos where the wardrobe was as loud as the bass.
We saw the trend crystallise in real time last month. Zendaya stepped out in a masterclass of sporty coordination, elevating a sleek, oversized half-zip anorak by pairing it with matching parachute trousers, proving that even a full kit can feel polished.
At the link in bio @patsyanthonyy’s roundup of styles that ensure you’re prepared for the elements without looking like you’re headed for a campsite.

posts.caption:
They say 13 is an unlucky number. To the Vaz family, it is anything but. It is the anniversary date of the launch of their liquor store (Vaz Enterprises) and the birth date of three of the women in the family. It somehow made sense that when they started their own space, it highlighted the number 13. Hence, Treize(@treize.goa ).
The newest bar to open in Goa, Treize occupies a small portion of Vaz Enterprises’ liquor store in Anjuna. It is Donovan Vaz’s(@vazdonovan ) fourth go at a bar after the popular Firefly(@firefly_goan_bistro_bar ), Tesouro(@tesouro.goa ), and SoGo(@barsogo ). This time, it is on his own turf—literally. Everything at Treize is his vision, and he takes the number seriously: there is seating for 13 people, and 13 cocktails on the menu.
At the link in bio, read @thatdoggonelady’s full review of the bar.

posts.caption:
Mindy Kaling’s latest show ‘Not Suitable for Work’ (NSFW)—aka Gen Z’s first workplace comedy—was announced as “five work-obsessed twenty-somethings who strive for professional success and, if they have time, personal happiness”. Yet, over the course of seven episodes, it hasn’t yet delivered on that premise. And with only the season finale left to air, it’s hard to know how they will make up for it and touch upon Gen Z and work, work-life balance, hustle culture...any of it.
Kaling’s ambitious early twenties were spent working on ‘The Office’, where she wrote more episodes than any other writer, engaged in a workplace situationship for the ages, and most importantly, gave us the iconic Kelly Kapoor. The Gen Z employee blueprint. So why is it that the characters on her latest show just can’t measure up? @saniyajafferrr shares her thoughts at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
If you are a dweller of the Maximum City—a student here on a summer internship, a hustler trying to make it big away from your hometown, or have always lived here— you need to read and savour Heirloom Cities’(@heirloomcities ) ‘Mumbai: A Journey Through Its Kitchens, Streets, and Stories’.
It’s not because it’s another art book that would make your coffee table look good. It’s also not because it’s the ultimate compendium of recipes that will take you from boiler of almost-perfect eggs to gourmet chef. Neither is it a ‘best places to eat at’ bible.
What it is, though, is a bit of everything...and then some more—like the city it is based on.
At the link in bio, @tanviii_05 with everything you need to know about the award-winning book.

posts.caption:
One morning, I woke up to an email telling me that Kai had been paralysed and skinned alive for a handbag. He had spent his life in a putrid pit, in a cesspool of faeces, fighting for space until the day he was immobilised with blunt force and killed.
Kai is a crocodile. The email came from People For Ethical Treatment of Animals (PETA).
This reminded me of one of fashion’s lingering contradictions. We celebrate craftsmanship and luxury, yet rarely ask what they sometimes cost. Despite avowals by many luxury brands to eliminate fur and exotic skins, the industry continues to depend on animal leather for handbags, shoes and accessories. Fashion journalism has made a promotional religion of sustainability, but animal welfare remains at the margins.
Every year, at events across the world, activists protest against the use of feathers, exotic skins and other animal-derived materials. They make headlines for a day but the questions they raise fade away quickly.
Since I am deeply distressed by animal cruelty, I first argued with myself before discussing this with the TVOF team. Was I biased?
Whether or not one agrees with every PETA campaign is beside the point. The questions surrounding leather deserve scrutiny. One of fashion’s most enduring defences is “ethically sourced leather”. Animal rights organisations argue there is no such thing. Leather is not merely a by-product of the meat industry, but a highly profitable industry in its own right. Reptiles are killed for their skins and bovine leather remains intertwined with cattle slaughter. The global leather goods market was estimated to be roughly $471 billion in 2024, almost matching the global beef market. Buying leather, thus, sustains an economic system that places commercial value on animal skins.
This is not to condemn everyone carrying a leather handbag. It is to invite compassion from an industry that has demonstrated ingenuity through recycled fibres, mushroom and pineapple leather and lab-grown materials.
June marks Save Their Skins Month. Perhaps it is time to ask questions about supply chains that bring animal leather to luxury ateliers.
Image 1 courtesy @peta

posts.caption:
Hi, I’m Veronica 👋🏼 a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio — each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. You’ll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.
The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.
The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.
Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .
Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .
Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.
For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com

posts.caption:
The Odyssey is named after a man, but read it again and you’ll find the women are the ones who actually run the show.
I’ve joined @readingsbooks Big Book Club working through Homer’s Odyssey. Five sessions with academics and authors, timed to Christopher Nolan’s adaptation landing in July. This is what I learned from Session 1 with Dr Seth Robinson.
Athena engineers the entire return. Circe gives Odysseus the knowledge to navigate the underworld. Nausicaa saves him on the beach. Ino saves him from drowning. Even Calypso hands him the tools to leave. The women are the ones who hold all the knowledge and the so-called monsters he defeats with it, Scylla, Charybdis, the Sirens, were only ever defending their own space.
And then there’s Penelope. Weaving by day, unpicking it by night, buying herself time with the one form of power she’s allowed…the loom and the sacred space it wields for her.
But the part that has stayed with me most was the translation. Robinson showed two versions of the same passage from Book 22, the killing of the maids. One translator calls them women, but Emily Wilson calls them girls. One frames it as deserved; the other shows you the pain. Translation is never an unbiased, neutral act. It will always tell you something about the person doing it, and the time they’re living in.
So here’s the question I’m sitting with: if this is the foundational text of the Western world, what does it say about us now?
Full essay on Substack 🔗 link in bio. Session 2 goes deeper into the women: Penelope, Helen, Calypso, Circe, Nausicaa.
What did you think of The Odyssey, if you’ve read it? Tell me below 👇🏼
In partnership with @readingsbooks

posts.caption: About textile crafts - magical power - women: When you have a look at the old fairytales of Germany, France and Northern Europe, these stories are also filled with that pattern. In the "Nibelungenlied" (the story of Sigfried the Dragonslayer, who gets to bath in dragonsblood which makes him uninjurable besides a very small spot on his shoulder and later gets killed through that spot by Hagen von Tronje) Krimhild, Sigfrieds Wife wants to protect him and markes the spot on his tunic with red thread. Also in the beginning of the story she and her women (she is a princess, so this is her household staff) are described to make lots of clothes for the men, also to embroider them. They did it for protection, also for presenting their social status.

posts.caption:
Every culture created a goddess who presided over making, but not all of them did it the same way.
This edition introduced two categories.
The first: goddesses whose mythology is explicitly about weaving (documented, primary sources, this is what the texts actually say).
The second: goddesses that weavers chose: figures of creativity, wisdom and skill that women at the loom reached toward deliberately, even though weaving wasn’t the centre of their story.
Both categories are true, just in different ways.
And here is what I keep coming back to across all three editions of the First Makers series. You never find a GOD of weaving. Not once, across any culture. Every time, the act of making cloth belongs to the feminine divine.
That is the oldest memory we have of what women’s knowledge meant.
Full episode on The Culture of Cloth podcast 🎧 link in bio. Start there if you’re new here.
Save this and share it with someone who makes things.
Cover art: Court Ladies Preparing Newly Woven Silk, a Chinese silk painting by Emperor Huizong of Song, early 12th century.

Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio — each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. You’ll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.

The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.

The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.

Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.

Stockists
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .

Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .

Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.

For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com

SEVENTEEN’s (@saythename_17) recent fan meeting was one of the group’s last major appearances as a near-complete lineup before more members begin their mandatory military service. With Jeonghan, Wonwoo, Hoshi and Woozi already enlisted, nine members took the stage, while Mingyu, DK, Seungkwan, Vernon and Dino are expected to follow in the coming months. As the group enters a new chapter, fans may find themselves revisiting old favourites and discovering new ones. To help, we’ve put together a playlist of standout SEVENTEEN tracks to keep you company. Did we miss any of your favourites? Let us know in the comments.
Video credits: SEVENTEEN on YouTube

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.
posts.alt_text: Madonna was among the front-row guests at @anthonyvaccarello’s latest menswear show for @ysl, joining Kate Moss, @connorstorrieofficial, @charli_xcx and more. Tap the link in our bio to see every look.

posts.caption:
In celebration of America's 250th anniversary, the @CFDA, @VogueMagazine, @RockefellerCenter, and @America250 present United Flags of Fashion. Leading American designers reimagined the flags of all 50 states, five U.S. territories, and Washington, D.C., creating original works that celebrate the creativity, diversity, and cultural richness of the United States.
Participating designers include Altuzarra, Amiri, APL, Bode, Calvin Klein, Carolina Herrera, Chloe Gosselin, Christopher John Rogers, Coach, Cult Gaia, Diotima, Donna Karan, Gabriela Hearst, Gap Inc., Kenneth Cole, Michael Kors, Ralph Lauren, Thom Browne, Tommy Hilfiger, Tory Burch, TWP, and Willy Chavarria.
Beginning July 27th, more than 120 custom flags will fly above Rockefeller Center through September 4th, transforming one of New York City's most iconic landmarks into a vibrant tribute to American fashion, culture, and design.  Learn more at the link in our bio. Photographed by @dbp_, Produced by @pinkproduction_carolinatakagi. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption:
Once a year, a special attraction descends on Coney Island, coaxing thousands of guests to gather along Surf Avenue leading toward the boardwalk to watch and participate in the historic Mermaid Parade. Founded in 1983 by Dick Zigun, the parade has served as the unofficial kickoff to the summer for over four decades; its procession culminates in a beach ceremony marking the opening of the ocean for the start of the summer swimming season. As it takes place during Pride Month, it has subsequently become an unofficial celebration of the queer community. The parade marchers dressed in a sea of fishnets, glitter, metallic scales, and neon wigs, but the crowd similarly brought their nautical A-game. See more from this year’s celebration at the link in bio
Photographed by @amirbangs.

posts.caption: For Father’s Day this year, Meghan, Duchess of Sussex, paid tribute to her husband, Prince Harry, with a sweet Instagram post that gives a rare glimpse into their family life. She captioned the post, “They’re so lucky to have you,” and added, “We all are” before wishing Harry a “Happy Father’s Day to our one and only.” Tap the link in our bio for more details. #Regram: @meghan
posts.alt_text:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.

She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”

Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.

For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile. 

Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption: While we're still over a month out from the release of "Spider-Man: Brand New Day," the red carpet fashions from @Zendaya and Tom Holland show no signs of slowing down. Starting the day in Berlin, Z opted for a sporty look reminiscent of one from Coach’s fall 2026 runway, and later traded in her daytime athletic apparel for a two-piece custom @LouisVuitton ensemble—the perfect complement to Tom's latest take on sharp tailoring in @celine. Head to the link in our bio for a look at every red carpet look so far from their first press tour as husband and wife.

posts.caption:
In celebration of America's 250th anniversary, the @CFDA, @VogueMagazine, @RockefellerCenter, and @America250 present United Flags of Fashion. Leading American designers reimagined the flags of all 50 states, five U.S. territories, and Washington, D.C., creating original works that celebrate the creativity, diversity, and cultural richness of the United States.
Participating designers include Altuzarra, Amiri, APL, Bode, Calvin Klein, Carolina Herrera, Chloe Gosselin, Christopher John Rogers, Coach, Cult Gaia, Diotima, Donna Karan, Gabriela Hearst, Gap Inc., Kenneth Cole, Michael Kors, Ralph Lauren, Thom Browne, Tommy Hilfiger, Tory Burch, TWP, and Willy Chavarria.
Beginning July 27th, more than 120 custom flags will fly above Rockefeller Center through September 4th, transforming one of New York City's most iconic landmarks into a vibrant tribute to American fashion, culture, and design.  Learn more at the link in our bio. Photographed by @dbp_, Produced by @pinkproduction_carolinatakagi. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption:
“Subtle shifts in perception: nuanced gestures that renew the collection with both ease and intention." This was how Leo Dell’Orco characterized the @GiorgioArmani manufacturing alchemy that produced shirts and loose unconstructed jackets that hit the eye like denim but had the touch, substance and material reality of shantung silk. posts.caption:
: ❤️✨ True Love vs Fake Love: 10 Signs You Should Know Before It’s Too Late
Love should bring peace, trust, and growth—not confusion and constant doubt. 💌 Sometimes, the difference between genuine love and a temporary attachment lies in the little things.
💖 True love supports you, respects your boundaries, and stays through life’s highs and lows.
💔 Fake love often thrives on convenience, control, and empty promises.
Knowing the signs can save you from heartbreak and help you recognize the kind of love you truly deserve. 🌷
Swipe through to discover the 10 signs that separate real love from fake love. Which one resonated with you the most? 👇
❤️ Save this post for later & share it with someone who needs to see this.

A stage. A story. A Rajkumari. 

RAJKUMARI is back, MY BIGGEST INDIA TOUR YET. (If you don’t see your city don’t worry, more cities to be added). 
Tickets on Book My Show.

What is Rajkumari?

An evening of stories, poems dipped in music. This is not just a show. It’s a gentle rebellion. Against everything you were told to be.A space to see differently.
To live differently. Your way. A reminder of the power of your own perspective, of your “Nayab Nazariya”.

After touring in 6 countries, Rajkumari is back in India. Come for one of India’s biggest storytelling & Poetry show.

Tickets on @bookmyshowin , link in my bio🔗

posts.caption:
✨ Creator, Actor, PHENOMENON! ✨ On the June-July cover of Wedding Vows, we present the trailblazing @mostlysane, Prajakta Koli!
From building a YouTube empire to being the lead for Netflix Original Mismatched, from being part of Michelle Obama’s Day Time Emmy winning docu-series to being UNDP India's First Youth Climate Champion, her journey is nothing short of extraordinary.
With her debut romance novel set to launch this year, along with her first Amazon Prime Original Andhera, Prajakta’s journey is a testament to the power of dreams, hard work, and staying true to oneself. She continues to inspire millions with her authenticity, relatability, and unstoppable spirit.

posts.caption:
@camimendes is having one of the biggest years in her career so far. A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto.
The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project.
In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.

stylist: @laurenegg

hair stylist: @laurenpalmersmith
makeup artist: @jentioseco
manicurist: @thuybnguyen
entertainment director: @jbake21
writer: @balencianas
producer: @_porcelainchaos @delafe_pr
special thanks to @blackwidowmustang & @goldlinebar
posts.alt_text:
There would be no Pope Heyward without @jonathandavissofficial. Much like his smart, careful, deeply moral Outer Banks character, Daviss knows what it feels like to carry the pressure of proving your worth and refusing to let yourself or anyone else down. “What really stood out to me about Pope in season 1 was this idea of expectations and pressure that gets put on a lot of young Black teens and teens in general,” he says. “There’s this pressure to succeed, and it was something I truly have been through.”

Daviss still thinks back to his early audition days before landing the life-changing role on OBX, when the constant need to prove himself as a young actor felt emotionally exhausting. His mindset then? “You only lose when you quit.”

Head to the link in our bio for the full download in our Now Playing Issue presented by @bvlgari, from what to expect from the fifth and final season of OBX to his upcoming portrayal of a young Snoop Dogg.

posts.caption:
@victoriamonet is trading her claws for butterfly wings. The three-time Grammy winner is the definition of a multi-hyphenate: a culinary school graduate, a mother, and, of course, a singer-songwriter with a nearly two-decade-spanning catalog to her name. Now, using her 37th birthday as a launching pad, Monét has cleared her mental space and scrubbed her socials to prepare for her next chapter—an album she’s dubbed her very own Thriller, a sonically surprising, genre-defying project that marks a softer evolution. “The jaguar exists within me always, but I’m feeling like a softer version of myself now,” she says.
After all, Monét’s critically acclaimed debut studio album Jaguar II was the ultimate learning lesson. “Once you have an accolade like that, people often look for a reason to discredit why you got it,” she explains. “I think—pending a Grammy happens again—I would do the opposite and bunker down in the studio.”
Step inside Monét’s world in our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, at the link in our bio.
